prompt:
Our board-games club runs a Kalah ladder by post and I keep getting disputes because everyone referees the sowing rules slightly differently. I want a single strict engine, class Kalah in Kalah.java, that both sides can run and get token-identical output. Plain Java, no libraries.

The setup: players A and B each own six pits and a store. A position fixture is three lines: 'turn A' (or B), then 'A p1 p2 p3 p4 p5 p6 | store', then the same for B. Each player's pits are written in their own sowing order, pit 1 first and pit 6 the one right before their store. Blank lines and surrounding spaces are fine; anything else malformed (wrong turn letter, wrong pit count, negative or non-numeric counts, missing the '|', rows missing/duplicated/out of order) must be rejected with IllegalArgumentException. Sample club positions are in positions/*.pos.

API I need on Kalah: static Kalah parse(String), String render() (exactly the three fixture lines back, 'turn -' once the game is over, no trailing newline), char turn(), boolean isOver(), String winner() ('A', 'B' or 'draw'; IllegalStateException while the game is running), String move(int pit), and static String transcript(String fixture, int[] moves).

Rules, the classic Kalah set: move(p) lifts every stone from your pit p (1-6; out-of-range or empty pit is IllegalArgumentException and must leave the board untouched; moving after the game ends is IllegalStateException) and sows them one per pit counter-clockwise: your higher pits, your store, then the opponent's pits 1 up to 6 — ALWAYS skipping the opponent's store — wrapping back to your pit 1 as often as needed (13 stones from a pit land the last one back where you started). If the last stone lands in your store you move again. If it lands in one of your own pits that was empty (the stone you just dropped is alone) and the opposite opponent pit — your pit k faces their pit 7-k — holds at least one stone, you capture: that last stone plus everything opposite goes into your store. Landing in an occupied pit of yours, or in any of the opponent's pits, captures nothing. After the move, whoever is due next must have at least one stone in their pits; if they have none, the game ends and the OTHER player sweeps all stones remaining on their own side into their own store (a sweep can be zero stones). Bigger store wins.

move() returns an event string: 'move', 'extra' or 'capture n' (n = stones banked by the capture including the landing stone), and when the move ends the game it becomes e.g. 'capture 4 / sweep A 3 / game over'.

transcript(fixture, moves) replays a numbered move list for the ladder archive: a '=== start ===' line and the initial render, then per move a header 'move N P pit p: <event>' (P = who moved) and the render after it, every block followed by one blank line, closing with 'result: winner A|B|draw' or 'result: next A|B', and every line newline-terminated. Any illegal move or bad fixture aborts with the exception — no partial transcript.

TestMain.java is the acceptance contract — 'java TestMain.java' must pass. Tests and the positions/ fixtures are read-only.
notes: 7G6 item 4: Kalah engine. All pinned boards/transcripts hand-computed before the reference implementation existed (reference then passed 29/29 first run). Pins: skip-opponent-store (11- and 13-stone sows), capture incl. the 13-stone wrap-capture edge, capture-only-when-opposite-nonempty, extra turn, sweep on stranded opponent (incl. zero-stone sweep and extra-turn-into-empty), winner/draw math, full transcript grammar. Domain check: no existing mancala/kalah seed in any language.
